title: Chicken Mushroom Arugula Risotto
ingredients: 1 tbsp olive oil (plus more for chicken)
Measure 3½ cups of hot water into a medium bowl.
Whisk in stock concentrate until combined. Set aside.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large wide saucepan over medium-high.
Add shallot and cook for 1 minute or until softened.
Add rice and cook for 1 minute, stirring consistently.
Add 2 cups of the prepared broth to the rice.
Bring to a low boil, reduce heat to medium and stir frequently to prevent burning.
Cook for 22–25 minutes, adding 1/2 cup broth at a time as the rice cooks and broth absorbs, until grains are tender and rice is creamy.
Drain diced chicken and pat dry with paper towel.
Heat 1 tbsp each butter and olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high.
Once butter is melted, add chicken and mushrooms and cook for 4–5 minutes, stirring occasionally, or until chicken is golden on the outside.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Stir in garlic and white wine. Use a wooden spoon to scrape any stuck browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
When half of the white wine has evaporated, turn off heat until ready to finish risotto.
When grains of rice are just tender, add the chicken and mushrooms into the saucepan.
Add parmesan and 2 tbsp butter, stirring until risotto is creamy and chicken is cooked to internal temperature of 165°F (74°C).
Stir in arugula for 1 minute or until just tender.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Divide risotto into large serving bowls.
markdown:
# Chicken Mushroom Arugula Risotto
tags: chicken, mushroom, arugula, risotto, arborio-rice, parmesan, white-wine, shallot, garlic, italian
time: 40min
serves: 2

## Ingredients
- 1 package diced chicken breast
- 200 g mushrooms
- 40 g arugula
- 3/4 cup arborio rice
- 50 g shallot
- 1 tbsp garlic
- 1/3 cup parmesan cheese
- approx. 1/3 cup white cooking wine
- 1 tsp chicken stock concentrate
- 1 tbsp olive oil (plus more for chicken)
- 2 tbsp + 1 tbsp butter
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Steps

### 1. Prepare Ingredients
- Measure 3½ cups of hot water into a medium bowl.
- Whisk in stock concentrate until combined. Set aside.
- Cut mushrooms into 1/4 inch slices.
- Roughly chop arugula.

### 2. Begin Cooking Rice
- Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large wide saucepan over medium-high.
- Add shallot and cook for 1 minute or until softened.
- Add rice and cook for 1 minute, stirring consistently.
- Add 2 cups of the prepared broth to the rice.
- Bring to a low boil, reduce heat to medium and stir frequently to prevent burning.
- Cook for 22–25 minutes, adding 1/2 cup broth at a time as the rice cooks and broth absorbs, until grains are tender and rice is creamy.

### 3. Cook Mushrooms + Chicken
- Drain diced chicken and pat dry with paper towel.
- Heat 1 tbsp each butter and olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high.
- Once butter is melted, add chicken and mushrooms and cook for 4–5 minutes, stirring occasionally, or until chicken is golden on the outside.
- Season to taste with salt and pepper.
- Stir in garlic and white wine. Use a wooden spoon to scrape any stuck browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
- When half of the white wine has evaporated, turn off heat until ready to finish risotto.

### 4. Finish Risotto
- When grains of rice are just tender, add the chicken and mushrooms into the saucepan.
- Add parmesan and 2 tbsp butter, stirring until risotto is creamy and chicken is cooked to internal temperature of 165°F (74°C).
- Stir in arugula for 1 minute or until just tender.
- Season to taste with salt and pepper.

### 5. Serve
- Divide risotto into large serving bowls.
- Enjoy!

title: Chicken Tikka Masala with Quinoa & Yogurt Cucumber Salad
ingredients: 7 tsp tikka masala spice blend (1 tsp ground cardamom, 1 1/2 tsp cumin, 1 1/2 tsp garam masala, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 1/2 tsp coriander)
Drain chicken and pat dry with a paper towel.
In a large bowl, combine one-third of the yogurt with half of the spice blend and 1/2 tsp salt.
Add chicken and mix well to coat. Set aside.
Rinse quinoa for 1 minute and drain in a fine-mesh sieve.
Add quinoa to a small pot with 1 cup salted water and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and cover with a lid. Cook for 18–20 minutes or until grains are tender.
Turn off heat and keep covered; let steam for 5 minutes.
Pick and chop cilantro. Trim ends off cucumber and dice into 1/2 inch pieces.
In a small bowl combine cucumber, remaining yogurt and cilantro.
Season to taste with salt and set aside.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large skillet over medium-high.
Add chicken and cook for 3–4 minutes, turning regularly, or until golden brown.
Remove from skillet and set aside.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in the same skillet over medium.
Once hot, add onion, garlic, ginger and tomato paste. Cook for 3 minutes or until softened.
Stir in remaining spice blend and red pepper flakes to taste for 30 seconds, or until fragrant.
Add diced tomatoes, coconut sugar and 1/2 cup water. Use a wooden spoon to scrape any stuck browned bits from bottom of pan.
Bring to a low boil and add cream. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Add chicken and cook for another 2–4 minutes until sauce has thickened and chicken reaches internal temperature of 165°F (74°C).
markdown:
# Chicken Tikka Masala with Quinoa & Yogurt Cucumber Salad
tags: chicken, tikka-masala, quinoa, cucumber, yogurt, cream, cilantro, tomato, onion, indian, spiced
time: 20min
serves: 2
source: FreshPrep

## Ingredients
- 1 package diced chicken breast
- 3/4 cup organic white quinoa
- 120 g cucumber
- 70 g onion
- 1 tbsp minced garlic
- 1 tbsp minced ginger
- 1/2 cup Greek yogurt
- 1/4 cup whipping cream
- 4 tsp tomato paste
- 3/4 cup diced tomatoes
- 1 tbsp coconut sugar
- 7 tsp tikka masala spice blend (1 tsp ground cardamom, 1 1/2 tsp cumin, 1 1/2 tsp garam masala, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 1/2 tsp coriander)
- 8 g fresh cilantro
- 1 tsp red pepper flakes
- olive oil
- salt and pepper to taste

## Steps

### 1. Prepare Chicken
- Drain chicken and pat dry with a paper towel.
- In a large bowl, combine one-third of the yogurt with half of the spice blend and 1/2 tsp salt.
- Add chicken and mix well to coat. Set aside.

### 2. Cook Quinoa
- Rinse quinoa for 1 minute and drain in a fine-mesh sieve.
- Add quinoa to a small pot with 1 cup salted water and bring to a boil.
- Reduce heat to low and cover with a lid. Cook for 18–20 minutes or until grains are tender.
- Turn off heat and keep covered; let steam for 5 minutes.

### 3. Prepare Cucumber Salad
- Pick and chop cilantro. Trim ends off cucumber and dice into 1/2 inch pieces.
- In a small bowl combine cucumber, remaining yogurt and cilantro.
- Season to taste with salt and set aside.

### 4. Cook Chicken
- Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large skillet over medium-high.
- Add chicken and cook for 3–4 minutes, turning regularly, or until golden brown.
- Remove from skillet and set aside.

### 5. Prepare Chicken Tikka
- Heat 1 tbsp oil in the same skillet over medium.
- Once hot, add onion, garlic, ginger and tomato paste. Cook for 3 minutes or until softened.
- Stir in remaining spice blend and red pepper flakes to taste for 30 seconds, or until fragrant.
- Add diced tomatoes, coconut sugar and 1/2 cup water. Use a wooden spoon to scrape any stuck browned bits from bottom of pan.
- Bring to a low boil and add cream. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
- Add chicken and cook for another 2–4 minutes until sauce has thickened and chicken reaches internal temperature of 165°F (74°C).

### 6. Serve
- Divide quinoa onto serving bowls.
- Top with chicken tikka.
- Garnish with cucumber salad.
- Enjoy!

## Nutrition
- Calories: 700 kcal
- Fat: 21 g
- Saturated Fat: 9 g
- Carbohydrates: 78 g
- Fibre: 9 g
- Sugars: 11 g
- Protein: 48 g
- Cholesterol: 135 mg
- Sodium: 280 mg
- Potassium: 1650 mg
- Calcium: 250 mg
- Iron: 8 mg

title: Creamy Mushroom Chicken with Potatoes, Asparagus & Gremolata
ingredients: Turn oven to 425°F (218°C). Line a baking sheet with foil.
Cut potatoes into 1/2 inch pieces and toss on baking sheet with 2 tbsp olive oil.
Season to taste with salt and pepper. Roast for 10 minutes.
Trim 2 inches off the ends of asparagus and discard.
Zest and cut lemon in half. Pick and finely chop parsley.
In a bowl with parsley, combine 1¼ tbsp olive oil, orange juice concentrate, almonds, one-third of the garlic, and both lemon zest and lemon juice to taste.
Season to taste with salt and red pepper flakes.
Once Step 1 timer goes off, toss asparagus with 1/2 tbsp olive oil on the baking sheet and season to taste with salt and pepper.
Return to oven for 12–15 minutes, or until vegetables are golden and tender.
Heat 1½ tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high.
Once hot, add chicken and cook for 2 minutes on one side. Flip chicken and push to one side.
Add mushrooms and onion. Cook for 2–4 minutes, stirring occasionally, until chicken and onions are golden.
Stir in remaining garlic and cook for 30 seconds, or until fragrant.
Add white wine vinegar and scrape bottom of skillet. Cook for 30 seconds, or until mostly evaporated.
Reduce heat to medium, stir in cream, 1 tsp herb blend, 1/2 cup water, crumbled stock cube and parmesan cheese.
Add remaining herb blend to taste.
Cook for 2–4 minutes until chicken reaches internal temperature of 165°F (74°C) and sauce has thickened.
Divide potatoes, asparagus and chicken onto serving plates.
markdown:
# Creamy Mushroom Chicken with Potatoes, Asparagus & Gremolata
tags: chicken, mushroom, asparagus, potato, cream, lemon, parsley, gremolata, garlic, freshprep, roasted
time: 30min
serves: 2
source: FreshPrep

## Ingredients
- 1 package diced chicken breast
- 300 g baby potatoes
- 100 g mushrooms
- 180g asparagus
- 60g onion
- 4 tsp minced garlic
- 1/3 cup cream
- 2 tsp orange juice concentrate
- 1 tbsp white wine vinegar
- 2 tbsp almonds
- 1/4 tsp mushroom stock cube
- 2 tbsp grated parmesan cheese
- 1 tsp herb blend
- 1/4 cup fresh parsley
- 1 lemon
- olive oil
- salt and pepper to taste

## Steps

### 1. Cook Potatoes
- Turn oven to 425°F (218°C). Line a baking sheet with foil.
- Cut potatoes into 1/2 inch pieces and toss on baking sheet with 2 tbsp olive oil.
- Season to taste with salt and pepper. Roast for 10 minutes.

### 2. Prepare Ingredients
- Trim 2 inches off the ends of asparagus and discard.
- Zest and cut lemon in half. Pick and finely chop parsley.
- Cut mushrooms into 1/4 inch slices.
- Drain and pat dry chicken.

### 3. Prepare Gremolata
- In a bowl with parsley, combine 1¼ tbsp olive oil, orange juice concentrate, almonds, one-third of the garlic, and both lemon zest and lemon juice to taste.
- Season to taste with salt and red pepper flakes.

### 4. Cook Asparagus
- Once Step 1 timer goes off, toss asparagus with 1/2 tbsp olive oil on the baking sheet and season to taste with salt and pepper.
- Return to oven for 12–15 minutes, or until vegetables are golden and tender.

### 5. Cook Chicken + Mushroom Sauce
- Heat 1½ tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high.
- Once hot, add chicken and cook for 2 minutes on one side. Flip chicken and push to one side.
- Add mushrooms and onion. Cook for 2–4 minutes, stirring occasionally, until chicken and onions are golden.
- Stir in remaining garlic and cook for 30 seconds, or until fragrant.
- Add white wine vinegar and scrape bottom of skillet. Cook for 30 seconds, or until mostly evaporated.
- Reduce heat to medium, stir in cream, 1 tsp herb blend, 1/2 cup water, crumbled stock cube and parmesan cheese.
- Add remaining herb blend to taste.
- Cook for 2–4 minutes until chicken reaches internal temperature of 165°F (74°C) and sauce has thickened.
- Season to taste with pepper.

### 6. Serve
- Divide potatoes, asparagus and chicken onto serving plates.
- Top chicken with mushroom sauce.
- Garnish with gremolata.

## Nutrition
- Calories: 650 kcal
- Fat: 28 g
- Carbohydrates: 58 g
- Fibre: 10 g
- Protein: 44 g
- Sodium: 1070 mg
- Potassium: 2050 mg
- Calcium: 300 mg
- Iron: 7 mg

title: Feta & Corn Fritters with Chipotle Mayo & Roasted Broccoli
ingredients: 1 tsp spice blend (1/2 tsp smoked paprika, 1/2 tsp roasted garlic powder)
Turn oven to 425°F (218°C). Line a baking sheet with foil.
Pick and chop cilantro. Thinly slice scallions.
Toss broccoli on baking sheet with 2 tbsp oil and garlic.
Bake for 15–17 minutes, or until broccoli is crisp and tender.
In a large bowl, whisk together buttermilk, eggs, baking powder and 1/2 tsp salt.
Stir in flour, corn, crumbled feta and scallions. Mix well.
Form mixture into 8 balls. Season to taste with salt. Wash hands.
Line a plate with paper towel.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high.
Carefully add fritter balls and reduce heat to medium. Cook for 3–4 minutes, then flip.
Using a spatula, press down lightly to flatten into 1-inch thick patties.
Cook for another 3–4 minutes until golden and crispy on the outside and cooked through the centre.
Transfer to lined plate to drain. Add additional oil between batches as needed.
In a small bowl, combine mayonnaise, chipotle spice blend and cilantro.
Season with salt, half of the lime juice and chipotle pepper to taste.
Add roasted broccoli and chipotle mayo on the side.
Add any remaining lime juice over broccoli if desired.
markdown:
# Feta & Corn Fritters with Chipotle Mayo & Roasted Broccoli
tags: feta, corn, fritters, broccoli, chipotle, mayo, lime, cilantro, scallion, garlic, vegetarian, pan-fried
time: 40min
serves: 2
source: FreshPrep

## Ingredients
- 400 g broccoli
- 1 1/2 cups corn kernels
- 1/2 cup feta cheese
- 1 free range egg
- 1/2 cup all-purpose flour
- 2 tbsp buttermilk
- 1 tsp baking powder
- 1/2 cup mayonnaise
- 1 tsp spice blend (1/2 tsp smoked paprika, 1/2 tsp roasted garlic powder)
- 1 tsp ground chipotle pepper
- 2 tbsp lime juice
- 2 tsp minced garlic
- 8 g cilantro
- 40 g green onion
- olive oil
- salt and pepper to taste

## Steps

### 1. Prepare Ingredients
- Turn oven to 425°F (218°C). Line a baking sheet with foil.
- Pick and chop cilantro. Thinly slice scallions.

### 2. Cook Broccoli
- Toss broccoli on baking sheet with 2 tbsp oil and garlic.
- Bake for 15–17 minutes, or until broccoli is crisp and tender.

### 3. Prepare Fritters
- In a large bowl, whisk together buttermilk, eggs, baking powder and 1/2 tsp salt.
- Stir in flour, corn, crumbled feta and scallions. Mix well.
- Form mixture into 8 balls. Season to taste with salt. Wash hands.

### 4. Cook Fritters
- Line a plate with paper towel.
- Heat 2 tbsp oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high.
- Carefully add fritter balls and reduce heat to medium. Cook for 3–4 minutes, then flip.
- Using a spatula, press down lightly to flatten into 1-inch thick patties.
- Cook for another 3–4 minutes until golden and crispy on the outside and cooked through the centre.
- Transfer to lined plate to drain. Add additional oil between batches as needed.

### 5. Prepare Chipotle Mayo
- In a small bowl, combine mayonnaise, chipotle spice blend and cilantro.
- Season with salt, half of the lime juice and chipotle pepper to taste.

### 6. Serve
- Divide fritters onto plates.
- Add roasted broccoli and chipotle mayo on the side.
- Add any remaining lime juice over broccoli if desired.
- Enjoy!

## Nutrition
- Calories: 850 kcal
- Fat: 57 g
- Saturated Fat: 12 g
- Carbohydrates: 68 g
- Fibre: 9 g
- Sugars: 9 g
- Protein: 24 g
- Cholesterol: 155 mg
- Sodium: 1120 mg
- Potassium: 1100 mg
- Calcium: 400 mg
- Iron: 4.5 mg

title: Italian Sausage Carbonara with Herbed Pangrattato & Broccolette
ingredients: Bring 2 litres salted water to a boil in a large pot.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high.
Once hot, add breadcrumbs and parsley and cook for 1–2 minutes until fragrant and golden.
Transfer into a bowl and set skillet aside.
Crack 2 whole eggs into a medium bowl.
For remaining eggs, add only yolks to same bowl, discarding whites or storing for another meal.
Add cream, parmesan, and coarse black pepper to taste and whisk until evenly combined.
Once water is boiling, add pasta and cook for 8–10 minutes or until al dente.
Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water, then drain pasta in a sieve and set aside for Step 5.
Trim 1 inch from stems of broccolette and cut any large stems in half lengthwise.
Line a baking sheet with foil. Combine broccolette with 2 tbsp olive oil and salt and pepper to taste.
Bake for 14–18 minutes until tender with crispy edges.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in the skillet from Step 1 over medium-high.
Add onion and cook for 1–2 minutes until golden.
Add sausage and garlic to skillet.
Cook for 4–5 minutes, breaking apart sausage with a wooden spoon, until meat reaches an internal temperature of 165°F.
Return cooked pasta to pot and remove from heat.
Add Italian sausage and onion mixture to the pot.
Slowly stir 1/4 cup reserved pasta water into the egg mixture, then add to pot.
Stir until pasta is evenly coated, adding remaining pasta water 1 tbsp at a time until desired sauce consistency is reached.
Divide pasta and broccolette into serving bowls and garnish with pangrattato.
markdown:
# Italian Sausage Carbonara with Herbed Pangrattato & Broccolette
tags: sausage, pasta, egg, broccolette, rigatoni, parmesan, italian, carbonara, baked
time: 40min
serves: 4
source: FreshPrep

## Ingredients
- 2 packages ground Italian sausage
- 280 g rigatoni
- 6 free range brown eggs
- 2 tbsp whipping cream
- 1/3 cup parmesan cheese
- 1/4 cup panko breadcrumbs
- 2 broccolette
- 100 g onion
- 1 tbsp garlic
- 8 g parsley
- 1 tsp coarse black pepper
- olive oil
- salt and pepper to taste

## Steps

### 1. Prepare Herbed Pangrattato
- Turn oven on to 425°F.
- Bring 2 litres salted water to a boil in a large pot.
- Pick and chop parsley.
- Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high.
- Once hot, add breadcrumbs and parsley and cook for 1–2 minutes until fragrant and golden.
- Transfer into a bowl and set skillet aside.

### 2. Prepare Egg Mixture
- Crack 2 whole eggs into a medium bowl.
- For remaining eggs, add only yolks to same bowl, discarding whites or storing for another meal.
- Add cream, parmesan, and coarse black pepper to taste and whisk until evenly combined.

### 3. Cook Pasta + Broccolette
- Once water is boiling, add pasta and cook for 8–10 minutes or until al dente.
- Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water, then drain pasta in a sieve and set aside for Step 5.
- Trim 1 inch from stems of broccolette and cut any large stems in half lengthwise.
- Line a baking sheet with foil. Combine broccolette with 2 tbsp olive oil and salt and pepper to taste.
- Bake for 14–18 minutes until tender with crispy edges.

### 4. Cook Sausage
- Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in the skillet from Step 1 over medium-high.
- Add onion and cook for 1–2 minutes until golden.
- Add sausage and garlic to skillet.
- Cook for 4–5 minutes, breaking apart sausage with a wooden spoon, until meat reaches an internal temperature of 165°F.

### 5. Finish Pasta
- Return cooked pasta to pot and remove from heat.
- Add Italian sausage and onion mixture to the pot.
- Slowly stir 1/4 cup reserved pasta water into the egg mixture, then add to pot.
- Stir until pasta is evenly coated, adding remaining pasta water 1 tbsp at a time until desired sauce consistency is reached.

### 6. Serve
- Divide pasta and broccolette into serving bowls and garnish with pangrattato.
- Enjoy!

title: Mexican Veggie Tortilla Lasagna with Mushroom Crumble Chili & Avocado
ingredients: 1 cup cheese blend (aged cheddar, monterey jack, mozzarella)
4½ tsp spice blend (1 tsp paprika, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp chili powder, 1 tsp oregano, ½ tsp coriander)
1 tbsp chipotle pepper in adobo sauce (spicy — use to taste)
Drain and rinse black beans in a sieve.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large pot over medium.
Once hot, add carrot, onion, and corn and cook for 3–4 minutes until tender.
Add ancho powder, chipotle in adobo to taste, tomato paste, garlic, and spice blend. Cook for 1–2 minutes until fragrant.
Add tomato sauce, mushroom crumble, black beans, and 1/2 cup water. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to medium and cook on a low boil for 5–7 minutes until slightly thickened.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Meanwhile, turn broiler on high to 500°F with a rack 6 inches from the top.
Cut avocado in half, remove pit, dice into 1/2 inch pieces and scoop flesh out with a large spoon.
Thinly slice green onion and add half of it to the chili.
Heat a large oven-proof skillet on low.
Add one-quarter of chili, then top with one tortilla.
Repeat until all tortillas are used, forming 3 layers.
Top with remaining chili and cheese blend.
Place skillet in oven and broil for 2–4 minutes until cheese is golden and bubbling.
Garnish with avocado and remaining green onion.
Serve family-style with sour cream and lime wedges on the side.
markdown:
# Mexican Veggie Tortilla Lasagna with Mushroom Crumble Chili & Avocado
tags: vegetarian, mushroom, tortilla, black-beans, corn, avocado, mexican, lasagna, baked, cheese
time: 35min
serves: 4
source: FreshPrep

## Ingredients
- 1 package mushroom crumble
- 1 can black beans
- 3 tortillas
- 190 g carrot and onion
- 3/4 cup corn
- 1 avocado
- 1½ cups tomato sauce
- 1 tbsp tomato paste
- 1 tbsp garlic
- 1 cup cheese blend (aged cheddar, monterey jack, mozzarella)
- 2/3 cup sour cream
- 4½ tsp spice blend (1 tsp paprika, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp chili powder, 1 tsp oregano, ½ tsp coriander)
- 1 tsp ground ancho powder
- 1 tbsp chipotle pepper in adobo sauce (spicy — use to taste)
- 30 g green onion
- 1 lime
- olive oil
- salt and pepper to taste

## Steps

### 1. Cook Chili
- Drain and rinse black beans in a sieve.
- Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large pot over medium.
- Once hot, add carrot, onion, and corn and cook for 3–4 minutes until tender.
- Add ancho powder, chipotle in adobo to taste, tomato paste, garlic, and spice blend. Cook for 1–2 minutes until fragrant.
- Add tomato sauce, mushroom crumble, black beans, and 1/2 cup water. Bring to a boil.
- Reduce heat to medium and cook on a low boil for 5–7 minutes until slightly thickened.
- Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.

### 2. Prepare Ingredients
- Meanwhile, turn broiler on high to 500°F with a rack 6 inches from the top.
- Cut avocado in half, remove pit, dice into 1/2 inch pieces and scoop flesh out with a large spoon.
- Cut lime into quarters.
- Thinly slice green onion and add half of it to the chili.

### 3. Begin Lasagna
- Heat a large oven-proof skillet on low.
- Add one-quarter of chili, then top with one tortilla.
- Repeat until all tortillas are used, forming 3 layers.
- Top with remaining chili and cheese blend.

### 4. Finish Lasagna
- Place skillet in oven and broil for 2–4 minutes until cheese is golden and bubbling.
- Monitor closely to prevent burning.

### 5. Serve
- Garnish with avocado and remaining green onion.
- Serve family-style with sour cream and lime wedges on the side.
- Enjoy!

title: Spicy Thai Chicken Breast with Peanut Chopped Salad & Crispy Noodles
ingredients: 3 tbsp soy blend (1 tbsp lime juice, 1 tbsp GF soy sauce, 1 tbsp brown sugar)
Zest and cut lime in half.
In a large bowl, whisk together peanut butter, sesame oil, soy sauce, fish sauce to taste and half of the garlic until smooth.
In a small bowl, whisk together lemongrass, remaining garlic, lime zest and lime juice to taste.
Drain chicken and pat dry with paper towel. Season with salt and pepper.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium.
Add chicken and cook for 6–8 minutes per side until golden.
Turn heat to low and wait 1 minute for skillet to cool slightly.
Add prepared glaze to skillet and cook for 1–2 minutes until thickened and chicken reaches internal temperature of 165°F (74°C).
Remove from skillet and let rest for 2–3 minutes.
To the large bowl with dressing, add cabbage, carrot, onion, spinach, basil and steam-fried noodles.
Toss to coat with dressing and divide into serving bowls.
Slice chicken into 1/3 inch thick slices and place on top of salad.
markdown:
# Spicy Thai Chicken Breast with Peanut Chopped Salad & Crispy Noodles
tags: chicken, thai, peanut, noodles, cabbage, carrot, spinach, basil, lime, soy, sesame, lemongrass, easy
time: 30min
serves: 2
source: FreshPrep

## Ingredients
- 1 package chicken breast
- 200 g shredded cabbage
- 65 g shredded carrot
- 40 g red onion
- 60 g spinach
- 8 g fresh basil
- 1 lime
- 15 g steam-fried noodles
- 3 tbsp roasted peanuts
- 2 tbsp peanut butter
- 1 tbsp lemongrass
- 1 tbsp minced garlic
- 3 tbsp soy blend (1 tbsp lime juice, 1 tbsp GF soy sauce, 1 tbsp brown sugar)
- 1 tbsp fish sauce
- 1 tbsp sesame oil
- olive oil
- salt and pepper to taste

## Steps

### 1. Prepare Ingredients
- Pick and roughly chop basil.
- Zest and cut lime in half.

### 2. Prepare Dressing
- In a large bowl, whisk together peanut butter, sesame oil, soy sauce, fish sauce to taste and half of the garlic until smooth.
- Set aside.

### 3. Prepare Glaze + Cook Chicken
- In a small bowl, whisk together lemongrass, remaining garlic, lime zest and lime juice to taste.
- Drain chicken and pat dry with paper towel. Season with salt and pepper.
- Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium.
- Add chicken and cook for 6–8 minutes per side until golden.

### 4. Glaze Chicken
- Turn heat to low and wait 1 minute for skillet to cool slightly.
- Add prepared glaze to skillet and cook for 1–2 minutes until thickened and chicken reaches internal temperature of 165°F (74°C).
- Remove from skillet and let rest for 2–3 minutes.

### 5. Finish + Serve
- To the large bowl with dressing, add cabbage, carrot, onion, spinach, basil and steam-fried noodles.
- Toss to coat with dressing and divide into serving bowls.
- Slice chicken into 1/3 inch thick slices and place on top of salad.
- Garnish with peanuts. Enjoy!

## Nutrition
- Calories: 550 kcal
- Fat: 27 g
- Saturated Fat: 4.5 g
- Carbohydrates: 36 g
- Fibre: 7 g
- Sugars: 15 g
- Protein: 44 g
- Cholesterol: 105 mg
- Sodium: 1650 mg
- Potassium: 1200 mg
- Calcium: 150 mg
- Iron: 4 mg

title: Sweet Chili Paneer with Scallion Rice & Roasted Broccoli
ingredients: 5 tbsp soy blend (2 tbsp soy sauce, 1½ tbsp coconut sugar, 1 tbsp ketchup, 2 tsp rice vinegar)
4 tbsp flour spice blend (2 tbsp cornstarch, 2 tbsp flour, ½ tsp each ground ginger, granulated garlic, black pepper, paprika)
Turn oven to 425°F (218°C). Line a baking sheet with foil.
Toss broccoli on baking sheet with 2 tbsp oil and season to taste with salt and pepper.
Rinse and drain rice in a fine-mesh sieve.
Add rice, coconut oil and half of the ginger to a small pot with 1¼ cups salted water and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and cover with a lid. Cook for 12–15 minutes or until grains are tender.
Cut bell pepper in half, remove stem and seeds, and dice into 1-inch pieces.
Thinly slice scallions. Drain paneer and pat dry with paper towel. Season with 1/2 tsp salt.
In a large bowl, whisk together 1/3 cup water, flour spice blend and red pepper flakes to taste.
Toss paneer in bowl with batter.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high.
Add paneer and cook for 3–4 minutes, turning regularly, until golden on all sides.
Remove from skillet and place on paper towel.
Heat same skillet over medium-high and add 1/2 tbsp oil if needed.
Add onion, bell pepper, all of the garlic and remaining ginger.
Cook for 3–4 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
Reduce heat to medium-low and stir in soy blend, 2 tbsp water and sweet chili sauce. Cook for 1 minute until sauce thickens, adding 1 tbsp water at a time to reach desired consistency.
Turn off heat. Add paneer and mix to coat with sauce.
Once rice is cooked, stir in scallions.
Divide rice into serving bowls and top with chili paneer and broccoli.
markdown:
# Sweet Chili Paneer with Scallion Rice & Roasted Broccoli
tags: paneer, broccoli, rice, bell-pepper, scallion, sweet-chili, soy, garlic, onion, vegetarian, roasted
time: 30min
serves: 2
source: FreshPrep

## Ingredients
- 1 package paneer
- 275 g broccoli
- 1 green bell pepper
- 20 g green onion
- 50 g red onion
- 3/4 cup basmati rice
- 1 tbsp butter
- 2 tsp minced garlic
- 4 tsp minced ginger
- 2 tbsp sweet chili sauce
- 5 tbsp soy blend (2 tbsp soy sauce, 1½ tbsp coconut sugar, 1 tbsp ketchup, 2 tsp rice vinegar)
- 4 tbsp flour spice blend (2 tbsp cornstarch, 2 tbsp flour, ½ tsp each ground ginger, granulated garlic, black pepper, paprika)
- 1 tbsp organic virgin coconut oil
- red pepper flakes to taste
- salt and pepper to taste

## Steps

### 1. Begin Broccoli
- Turn oven to 425°F (218°C). Line a baking sheet with foil.
- Cut broccoli into 1-inch pieces.
- Toss broccoli on baking sheet with 2 tbsp oil and season to taste with salt and pepper.
- Roast for 20–22 minutes.

### 2. Begin Rice
- Rinse and drain rice in a fine-mesh sieve.
- Add rice, coconut oil and half of the ginger to a small pot with 1¼ cups salted water and bring to a boil.
- Reduce heat to low and cover with a lid. Cook for 12–15 minutes or until grains are tender.

### 3. Prepare Ingredients
- Cut bell pepper in half, remove stem and seeds, and dice into 1-inch pieces.
- Thinly slice scallions. Drain paneer and pat dry with paper towel. Season with 1/2 tsp salt.
- In a large bowl, whisk together 1/3 cup water, flour spice blend and red pepper flakes to taste.

### 4. Cook Paneer
- Toss paneer in bowl with batter.
- Heat 2 tbsp oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high.
- Add paneer and cook for 3–4 minutes, turning regularly, until golden on all sides.
- Remove from skillet and place on paper towel.

### 5. Cook Chili Paneer
- Heat same skillet over medium-high and add 1/2 tbsp oil if needed.
- Add onion, bell pepper, all of the garlic and remaining ginger.
- Cook for 3–4 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
- Reduce heat to medium-low and stir in soy blend, 2 tbsp water and sweet chili sauce. Cook for 1 minute until sauce thickens, adding 1 tbsp water at a time to reach desired consistency.
- Turn off heat. Add paneer and mix to coat with sauce.

### 6. Finish + Serve
- Once rice is cooked, stir in scallions.
- Divide rice into serving bowls and top with chili paneer and broccoli.
- Enjoy!

## Nutrition
- Calories: 870 kcal
- Fat: 32 g
- Carbohydrates: 116 g
- Fibre: 8 g
- Sugars: 27 g
- Protein: 33 g
- Cholesterol: 85 mg
- Sodium: 1210 mg
- Potassium: 1000 mg
- Calcium: 1000 mg
- Iron: 5 mg
